stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of body weight using a bathroom scale. The measurements have a mean of 48.0 kg and a standard deviation of 7.1 kg. However, calibration tests reveal that a bathroom scale systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.8% and 7.6%. Estimate the true body weight and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 48.0 kg. After correcting for the 6.2% bias: ≈ 51.2 kg. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.8%-7.6%) and sampling variability: [45.9, 56.4]. Point estimate: 51.2 kg.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
